name: azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter-java
description: Export OpenTelemetry traces, metrics, and logs to Azure Monitor and Application Insights using Java.
capabilities: Generate Java code for OpenTelemetry SDK autoconfiguration with Azure Monitor
Create distributed tracing spans with custom attributes and exception handling
Set up metrics collection using OpenTelemetry counters and histograms
Configure custom span processors for telemetry customization
Provide migration guidance from deprecated packages to autoconfigure
Document environment variable setup for Application Insights connection
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill consists entirely of Markdown documentation (SKILL.md) with Java code examples for Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry integration. The 26 'external_commands' detections are Markdown backtick delimiters for code blocks, not shell execution. The 10 'network' findings are documentation URLs (GitHub, Microsoft Learn, Maven Central), not actual network calls. No executable code or security risks present.
valueStatement: Integrate Java applications with Azure Monitor for distributed tracing and telemetry. Use OpenTelemetry standards to export traces, metrics, and logs to Application Insights.
userTitle: Export OpenTelemetry telemetry to Azure Monitor
limitations: The referenced azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter package is deprecated
Requires Azure Application Insights connection string for configuration
Documentation only - does not include executable library code

useCases.targetUser: DevOps engineers managing Java workloads on Azure
useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Monitoring Java Applications
useCases.description: Set up end-to-end distributed tracing for microservices running on Azure. Configure OpenTelemetry to automatically export traces and metrics to Application Insights for production monitoring.

useCases.title: Java Developer Implementing Observability
useCases.description: Add custom instrumentation to Java applications using OpenTelemetry APIs. Create meaningful spans, record exceptions, and capture business metrics for debugging and performance analysis.

useCases.title: Cloud Architect Designing Telemetry Strategy
useCases.description: Evaluate OpenTelemetry integration patterns for Azure monitoring. Compare autoconfiguration versus manual setup approaches for enterprise Java application portfolios.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Azure Monitor Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up OpenTelemetry to export telemetry to Azure Monitor. I have a Java Spring Boot application and an Application Insights connection string.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner: First-time integration with Azure Monitor

promptTemplates.title: Custom Tracing Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create custom spans with attributes in my Java application using OpenTelemetry. I need to trace order processing with order ID and customer tier attributes.
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate: Adding custom instrumentation to existing code

promptTemplates.title: Migration from Deprecated Package
promptTemplates.prompt: My application uses azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter which is deprecated. Guide me through migrating to azure-monitor-opentelemetry-autoconfigure with code examples.

promptTemplates.title: Advanced Span Processing
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to implement a custom SpanProcessor that adds organization-specific attributes to all spans and filters out health check endpoints. Show me the complete implementation.

bestPractices: Use the autoconfigure package instead of the deprecated direct exporter for simplified setup
Always end spans in finally blocks or use try-with-resources for proper Scope management
Record exceptions on spans using recordException() and set appropriate error status codes
antiPatterns: Using the deprecated azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter package for new projects
Hardcoding connection strings in source code instead of using environment variables
Creating spans without proper exception handling or failing to end spans in error cases

faq.question: Is the azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter package still supported?
faq.answer: No, this package is deprecated. Microsoft recommends migrating to azure-monitor-opentelemetry-autoconfigure which provides automatic instrumentation and simplified configuration.

faq.question: How do I get an Application Insights connection string?
faq.answer: Create an Application Insights resource in the Azure Portal. Navigate to the resource and find the connection string under 'Configure' section. It includes the instrumentation key and ingestion endpoint.

faq.question: Can I use OpenTelemetry with existing Application Insights SDK?
faq.answer: Yes, but Microsoft recommends migrating fully to OpenTelemetry for new development. The autoconfigure package provides compatibility while following OpenTelemetry standards.

faq.question: What telemetry data can I export to Azure Monitor?
faq.answer: OpenTelemetry supports exporting traces (distributed tracing spans), metrics (counters, histograms, gauges), and logs to Azure Monitor/Application Insights.

faq.question: Do I need to install the Azure SDK for Java?
faq.answer: You need the azure-monitor-opentelemetry-autoconfigure Maven package. The OpenTelemetry Java SDK handles the core instrumentation, while Azure package provides the exporter.

faq.question: How does distributed tracing work with OpenTelemetry and Azure?
faq.answer: OpenTelemetry automatically propagates trace context across service boundaries using W3C trace context headers. Each span is exported to Application Insights where you can view end-to-end transaction flows.
